LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT SQUARE D COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MATTER WAS ARGUED. TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF LACKS EVIDENCE OF EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS FOR WHICH DEFENDANT IS RESPONSIBLE AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT ON THAT ISSUE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IS OVERRULED. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THE COHEN DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF FOUNDATION TO THE EXTENT HE TESTIFIES AS TO EITHER PLAINTIFF'S CREATING DUST ON THE JOB OR THE ASBESTOS CONTENT OF MATERIALS WITH WHICH PLAINTIFF WORKED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189
